scripts.name: Limb-a-tron

consumes.minimum: 1 active composition (no layers selected for creation mode; a Limb-a-tron controller or shape layer selected for bake mode)
consumes.description: Creation mode: reads the active CompItem for dimensions (width/height) to place the shape layer and controller null at comp centre. Bake mode: reads the selected layer(s) to locate the LimbControlsV3 pseudo-effect, then traverses the full parent-chain and all keyframes on the controller null and shape layer.

controls.name: Limb Name
controls.role: sets the name used for the shape layer, controller null, and pseudo-effect instance; also injected into every expression string at build time

controls.name: OK
controls.role: confirms limb name entry and triggers rig creation

controls.name: Cancel
controls.role: dismisses the name dialog without creating a rig

controls.name: Yes (bake confirm)
controls.role: confirms bake-and-remove-controller operation on a selected Limb-a-tron rig

controls.name: No (bake confirm)
controls.role: dismisses the bake confirmation dialog without baking

functions: guards when no active composition is present
guards when selected layers are not a Limb-a-tron rig
shows a name-input dialog for new rig creation
creates a named shape layer with Regular Limb and Noodle Limb vector groups
creates a controller null layer with a LimbControlsV3 pseudo effect applied from a temp FFX binary
wires IK-angle, adjusted-length, and pop-prevent expressions onto the controller null pseudo-effect sliders
wires elbow and wrist comp-space / layer-space position expressions onto the controller null
wires shape-path expressions (regular and noodle body) onto the shape layer vector groups
wires fill color and fill opacity expressions linked to the controller pseudo-effect
wires linked stroke-pass expressions (color, width, trim start/end/offset) for regular and noodle render passes
wraps all mutations in a named undo group
detects existing Limb-a-tron rigs from selected layers via pseudo-effect introspection
shows a bake-confirm dialog when a rig is selected
bakes animated body-path expressions to per-frame keyframes with Hermite simplification
freezes static appearance properties (fill color, stroke width, trim ranges) as static values
removes inactive render group (Regular or Noodle) after baking based on Noodle Mode state
optionally removes the controller null layer after a confirmed bake

sim.note: Samples the elbow layer-space position over the composition duration, showing how the elbow point tracks the IK solution as the controller is animated.

sim.note: Samples the wrist layer-space position over the composition duration, tracking the IK end-effector position.

sim.note: Reads Arm Fill Color from the controller pseudo-effect directly; result is constant unless the control is keyframed.

expressions.target: Arm/ADBE Root Vectors Group/Regular Limb/ADBE Vectors Group/ADBE Vector Graphic - Fill/ADBE Vector Fill Opacity
expressions.expression: thisComp.layer('Arm CTRL').effect('Arm Limb Control')('Arm Fill Opacity')

sim.note: Sweeps Arm Fill Opacity 0–100 to confirm the fill opacity passes through linearly from the controller slider.

notes: The script runs in two exclusive modes: creation mode (no layers selected) and bake mode (Limb-a-tron rig selected). The same dialog/entry point dispatches both.

The LimbControlsV3 pseudo effect is shipped as a compiled binary (base-64 in the JSX) written to a temp .ffx file and applied via applyPreset — this requires 'Allow Scripts to Write Files' in AE preferences.

All expression strings are built at rig-creation time with the limb name interpolated directly into the source, so every expression references its controller null and shape layer by the exact name chosen in the dialog.
Bake mode collects every keyframe time across the full parent chain (up to 4 levels), resamples to per-frame times, then uses Hermite simplification (shapeError threshold) to prune redundant keys.

The regularLimbExpression builds a dense sampled polyline with cap arcs, elbow arc, and side-curvature bell curves — vertex count is fixed at (CAP_STEPS+1)+SIDE_STEPS+ELBOW_STEPS+SIDE_STEPS+CAP_STEPS+SIDE_STEPS+ELBOW_STEPS+SIDE_STEPS to keep the closed path topologically stable across all poses.
Noodle mode and Regular mode are mutually exclusive: the active mode path expression returns a real shape while the inactive path returns an empty fixed-size zero path.

scriptSim.scenario: success – 1D opacity linked to slider driver
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 10s); driver layer 'Driver Null' with a Slider Control effect (value 50); target layer 'Target Shape' with Opacity keyed at t=0→0%, t=2→50%, t=4→100%; Opacity pre-selected (batch mode); user selects driver Slider, enters Min=0 Max=100, clicks Apply

expressions.target: Target Shape/Transform/Opacity

sim.note: Sweeps the Slider Control value from 0 to 100 (minVal→maxVal); output is the linearly interpolated Opacity value (0%→50%→100%) using the 3-keyframe piecewise mapping with relative time distribution (t=0→t=2→t=4 maps to segments [0,50] and [50,100]).

notes: UI mode is declared DIALOG in @ui frontmatter but the live window is created with Window('palette') — the harness treats it as DIALOG per frontmatter.
Batch mode activates automatically when properties are pre-selected at script launch; single interactive mode is used otherwise.
The checkbox controller (ADBE Checkbox Control) is added to the driver layer and controls even-vs-relative interpolation from inside the generated expression.
Hold keyframes are not supported and will silently produce incorrect results.
3D/4D target properties are rejected with an alert at target-select time.
For multi-dimensional driver properties a secondary sub-dialog (Window 'dialog') prompts for X/Y/Z/W dimension selection; this sub-dialog is not exposed as a top-level control here.
Expressions are generated as ECMA 3-compatible strings using a custom serializeForExpression serializer (no JSON.stringify).
Shape path (SHAPE) targets produce a different expression body using linearArrayInterpolation over vertex/tangent arrays; the fixture success scenario covers the 1D (Opacity) case only.

collect all shape layers (those with a Contents group) from the active comp

scriptSim.scenario: success – animate along shape path with ease-in-out
scriptSim.inputs: Composition 'Scene Comp' (1920×1080, 24 fps, 10 s) containing shape layer 'Path Shape' (Group 1 > Path 1) and AV layer 'Animated Layer' with its Position property selected

expressions.target: Animated Layer/Transform/Position

sim.note: Sweeps time from 0 to 2 s (the defaultDuration for a 48-frame animation at 24 fps) to plot the layer's progress along the path under Ease-In-Out easing (0.42, 0, 0.58, 1) with no loop, full segment 0–100%, and reverseDir=false

notes: The script runs in a strict three-dialog sequence (shape-layer list → path tree → easing/duration). Cancelling any dialog exits without making changes.

The ScriptUI listbox and dropdownlist are populated via the positional 3rd argument of add(); the harness scriptui root ignores that form and creates empty lists, so the harness cannot reach the success branch without a fix to the scriptui root's Control.add() handling.

The Reverse Direction checkbox is implemented as an ADBE Checkbox Control effect named 'Reverse Direction' added to the layer; the expression reads it at runtime via effect("Reverse Direction")("Checkbox").

Easing values are embedded in the Animation Easing marker comment using the pattern 'Animation Easing: p1x,p1y,p2x,p2y || Segment start - end%'; if the marker is absent or malformed the expression falls back to linear [0,0,1,1].
The expression supports three loop modes: none (clamp), loop (modulo), and pingpong (back-and-forth); the mode is read from the marker comment at runtime.
For parented layers the expression converts the path position through toComp() then back through parent.fromComp() to stay correct in local space.
durationSeconds is baked into the expression source at write-time as a numeric literal (e.g. 2 for 48 frames at 24 fps); it also sets the marker duration so both stay in sync.

expressionCurves.target: Animated Layer/Transform/Position

notes: Screenshot: 275x238 px (logical, device scale factor 2).

Expression curve: 41 points over time [0, 2] s. No Animation Easing marker present → expression falls back to linear [0,0,1,1] easing, full path segment 0–100%, no loop. Output is x-position along a synthetic straight-line path (0→1920 px). The cubic-bezier, loop, ping-pong, and reverse-direction branches are exercised by marker comment parsing at AE runtime.

scripts.name: Guiderator

consumes.minimum: 1 active composition (CompItem)
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem to obtain the target CompItem. Also reads app.activeViewer.views[0].options.guidesLocked to reflect the current lock state on the lock button. No layer or keyframe selection is required.

controls.role: accepts a pixel position or arithmetic expression (e.g. '1920/2') which is evaluated and used as the guide coordinate

controls.role: adds a horizontal guide at the evaluated input position (calls comp.addGuide(0, value))

controls.name: V
controls.role: adds a vertical guide at the evaluated input position (calls comp.addGuide(1, value))

controls.name: Lock
controls.role: toggles the guide-locked state of the active viewer (view.options.guidesLocked); label shows 🔐 when locked, 🔓 when unlocked

functions: evaluate arithmetic expression entered in the position input field
add a horizontal guide to the active composition at the specified pixel position
add a vertical guide to the active composition at the specified pixel position
toggle the guide-locked state of the active viewer
alert the user when the input is empty, non-numeric, or no composition is active

scriptSim.scenario: success — add horizontal guide at 540
scriptSim.inputs: Active composition 'Main Comp' (1920×1080, 30 fps, 10 s); no layers selected; position input set to '540'; H button clicked

notes: comp.addGuide() is not an OPERATION_KIND; the harness cannot intercept it — success scenarios have expectedConfidence 'low'.
The position input uses eval() internally to resolve arithmetic expressions before addGuide is called; the result is rounded to 2 decimal places.
The lock button reads app.activeViewer.views[0].options.guidesLocked; if no viewer is present the label defaults to 🔐 and the toggle is a no-op.
addGuide(0, value) adds a horizontal guide; addGuide(1, value) adds a vertical guide — the axis argument is the inverse of the button label direction.
Script targets ES3 (ECMA3) — no arrow functions, let/const, or template literals.
Script ui mode is PANEL — UITree was captured from the success scenario (add horizontal guide at 540) via runFixtureScenario.
PNG rendered via SDB service at 152×160 px (15 566 bytes); file is sane.
expressions[] is empty — no AE expressions are written by this script; expressionCurves is empty by design.
comp.addGuide() is not an OPERATION_KIND; the harness intercepts only the surrounding beginUndoGroup / endUndoGroup pair. expectedConfidence is 'low' for success scenarios.
The lock button icon (🔐) renders correctly in the captured UITree — label defaults to locked state as no viewer is present in the harness host.

notes: HEADLESS script — no ScriptUI dialog; runs entirely via app.beginUndoGroup/endUndoGroup.
prompt() is called exactly once (via getPrefix()) to collect a layer-name prefix; if cancelled or left empty the prefix is an empty string and layer names use their default 'S- …' identifiers.
Layer-name renaming is deferred: layers are first created with 'name:…' prefixes, then batch-renamed at the end via app.project.autoFixExpressions to keep expression references consistent.
All expression references use the literal layer name 'name:S- PiP MASK PSR'; after autoFixExpressions runs these are updated to the final prefixed name.
S- PiP Mask Shadow and S- PiP Mask are initially created then locked after expressions are applied.
Several layers (S- Mask Bottom, S- Mask Top, S- PiP Mask Shadow, S- Z < …>) are created in disabled state (enabled = false) as template infrastructure.
The script depends on AE-runtime-only constructors: MarkerValue, Shape, CompItem, FolderItem — cannot be replicated in a pure-JS harness without mocks.
Effect controls (Mask Size X/Y, PiP Shadow Opacity/Distance/Softness/Color, Pip Shadow Angle) live on the S- PiP MASK PSR shape layer as AE effect instances, not ScriptUI widgets.
setParentWithJump is used (preserves world position) rather than plain setParent.

All parameters support keyframe animation, but the script writes only two keyframe pairs (Trim Start/End) per stroke layer.

scripts.name: ChromaBlenderizer

consumes.minimum: 1 active CompItem + 2 selected COLOR-type properties
consumes.description: Reads app.project.activeItem (must be a CompItem) at script launch and again at apply time. At apply time reads comp.selectedProperties and filters to those whose propertyValueType === PropertyValueType.COLOR. Requires at least 2 such color properties to proceed.

controls.name: Start Color
controls.role: opens AdobeColorPicker to set the range start color; updates config.startHue, config.saturation, config.startBrightness

controls.name: End Color
controls.role: opens AdobeColorPicker to set the range end color; updates config.endHue, config.saturation, config.endBrightness

controls.name: Start Hue
controls.role: sets the hue of the first generated color in Color Gamut mode (0–1 maps to 0–360 degrees)

controls.name: End Hue
controls.role: sets the hue of the last generated color in Color Gamut mode (0–1 maps to 0–360 degrees)

controls.name: Shared Saturation
controls.role: sets the saturation shared by both start and end colors in Color Gamut mode (0–1)

controls.name: Start Brightness
controls.role: sets the brightness of the first generated color (0–1)

controls.name: End Brightness
controls.role: sets the brightness of the last generated color (0–1)

controls.name: Randomize Colors
controls.role: when checked, assigns each target a random color within the start/end range instead of a linearly interpolated one

controls.name: Grayscale
controls.role: selects Grayscale mode: ignores hue and saturation, interpolates or randomizes brightness only

controls.name: Color Gamut
controls.role: selects Color Gamut mode: uses hue (shortest-path interpolation), shared saturation, and brightness to generate colors

controls.name: Refresh Count
controls.role: counts currently selected AE color properties and updates the property-count display without applying changes

controls.name: What Counts?
controls.role: shows an informational alert describing which AE property types qualify as color targets

controls.name: Apply Colors
controls.role: applies the generated color range to all currently selected color properties using beginUndoGroup/endUndoGroup

controls.name: Close
controls.role: closes the modeless palette

functions: validate active composition on launch and abort with alert if none found
open modeless palette that remains visible while user selects AE properties
accept start/end color via bundled AdobeColorPicker swatch dialog
set start/end hue, shared saturation, and per-color brightness via HSB sliders
count and display selected COLOR-type properties on demand (Refresh Count)
interpolate colors linearly across selected color properties using shortest-path hue interpolation
distribute random colors within the start/end range across selected color properties
generate grayscale values by interpolating brightness with zero saturation
generate color-gamut values using HSB-to-RGB conversion
apply computed RGB values to each selected color property via property.setValue()
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'ChromaBlenderizer'

scriptSim.scenario: success – linear color gamut applied to 2 fill color properties
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Chroma Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 10s) with two shape layers. Shape A Fill Color (red [1,0,0,1]) and Shape B Fill Color (blue [0,0,1,1]) are both in comp.selectedProperties. All config defaults: startHue=0, endHue=0, saturation=0, startBrightness=1, endBrightness=1 (both colors will be white). User clicks Apply Colors.

notes: PALETTE mode: the window is modeless — AE remains interactive while the script panel is open, allowing users to change their color-property selection between Apply passes.
The script writes no AE expressions; all color values are applied as static setValue() calls.
Start/End color defaults are white ([1,1,1]) because startHue=0, endHue=0, saturation=0, startBrightness=1, endBrightness=1 yields hsbToRgb(0,0,1)=[1,1,1,1].
Hue interpolation uses shortest-path (wraps through ±0.5 boundary) so e.g. startHue=0.9, endHue=0.1 travels through red rather than the long way around.
Randomize mode uses Math.random() so results are non-deterministic; the fixture tests linear (deterministic) mode only.
The Grayscale radio forces h=0, s=0 regardless of the HSB sliders; only brightness is interpolated.
A singleton guard prevents duplicate palette windows: if $.global.ChromaBlenderizerPalette already exists the old window is closed before a new one opens.
The bundled AdobeColorPicker (Smallpath v2.0) is inlined into the script as a vendored dependency; if it is unavailable the swatch buttons fall back to an alert directing the user to the HSB sliders.
endUndoGroup target is listed as 'ChromaBlenderizer' for fixture consistency; the actual AE call is app.endUndoGroup() with no argument.
UI mode is PALETTE; uiTree captured from the success scenario (Apply Colors clicked with default white-to-white color gamut settings).
PNG rendered via SDB service: 412x464 px (CSS), 824x928 px at device scale factor 2, 39 SDB items.
PNG size: 47446 bytes — well above 1 KB threshold; dimensions sane.
expressions[] is empty — ChromaBlenderizer applies static setValue() calls only and writes no AE expressions; expressionCurves is therefore [].
Operations log had 7 entries: beginUndoGroup, 2x alert (property-count display on launch), setValue x2, endUndoGroup — consistent with success scenario expectations.

consumes.minimum: 1 or more compositions selected in the Project panel
consumes.description: Reads app.project.selection — expects one or more CompItem entries. For each comp it iterates all layers, identifying audio-only FootageItem layers (hasVideo === false) and nested CompItem layers (processed recursively). Inspects each audio layer's Marker property to classify it as Voice Over, Music, or Sound Effects. Also checks whether any audio layer already has a Stereo Mixer effect with expressions so it can prompt before overwriting.

The expr-eval runtime cannot return values from if/else block branches (AE expression dialect constraint), so curves are computed directly from the deterministic linear() formula that the active branch resolves to in the single-parent case.

Scale curve uses the proportional adjustment path: since parent1Scale=[200,200] and parent2Layer=null, adjustedScaleX = (100/200)*200 = 100, which equals originalScale, so the blended result is the same as the simple linear interpolation from 100 to 200.

Depends on three //@include directives (RefManager, ApplyFFX, rectangulator_expressions.js) that must be bundled or available at runtime; the script is KNOWN-BLOCKED in standalone Node execution.

The 'Rectangulator Controls' pseudo-effect parameter names in the binary use short aliases ('Width', 'Height', 'Top Left Roundness %', etc.) which differ from the UI label strings ('Rectangle Width (px)', etc.); expressions reference the short alias form.

The position expression uses a stateful __state object to track delta changes between evaluations; this pattern works in AE's expression engine but cannot be accurately swept in a stateless evaluator.

Corner rounding sliders are percentage-based (0–100%) and are internally converted to pixel values relative to Math.min(width, height); adjacent-corner over-sum constraints prevent intersecting bezier curves.
The original parametric rectangle group is removed (or disabled as a fallback) after conversion; the operation is wrapped in a single undo group named 'Rectangulator'.

Original AE expressions use bare (var-less) assignments that rely on sloppy-mode global scope; the expr-eval "use strict" sandbox causes them to throw inside the catch block, returning the fallback path. Clean adapter expressions reproduce the same output logic using explicit var declarations.
Expression[0] adapter sweeps width 0–1000 px (height=200, all corners 0%, anchor=Center); plots first bezier vertex x = −width/2, which varies linearly from 0 to −500.
Expression[1] adapter steps anchorType 1–9 (width=200, height=100); the x component of the anchor offset is −100 for left column, 0 for center column, +100 for right column.

Expression[2] adapter sweeps width 0–1000 with anchor=Center; position x compensation is −width/2 (linear from 0 to −500), keeping the rectangle visually pinned at center. The original is stateful (delta-based); this adapter shows the equivalent cumulative shift from starting position (0,0).

show a DIALOG prompting the user to enter a case-sensitive search term

notes: Text search is strictly case-sensitive; there is no option to make it case-insensitive.
Carriage-return characters (char code 13) are excluded from character index calculations so that Range Selector indices match AE's character-level units.
Color selection rotates through a fixed palette of 12 high-contrast RGB colors; consecutive highlights are guaranteed to use different colors via lastColorIndex tracking.
All existing text animations, keyframes, and formatting are preserved — the script only adds new animators.
The script writes no AE expressions; expressions[] is empty.

build a linear-interpolation expression string that maps a 0–100 slider value to a time range defined by the 'In/Out Range' point control divided by the composition frame rate
write the generated expression onto the layer's 'ADBE Time Remapping' property

sim.note: Sweeps the Slider Control value from 0 (→ outputMin = inOutRange[0]/24 = 0s) to 100 (→ outputMax = inOutRange[1]/24 = 2.5s at 24fps with default [0,60]). Output is the linearly remapped time in seconds fed to the ADBE Time Remapping property, controlling playback position across the in/out frame range.

notes: Time remapping must be manually enabled on the source layer before running the script (canSetTimeRemapEnabled check is passive — the script does not enable it automatically).
The expression embeds the controller layer name and effect name as literal strings at generation time; renaming either layer or effect after application will break the expression.
The 'In/Out Range' Point Control stores frame numbers (not seconds); the expression divides by the composition frameRate (embedded as a literal at generation time) for AE time-in-seconds output.
The slider operates on a fixed 0–100 input domain regardless of the actual In/Out frame range; the range is entirely governed by the Point Control values.
If a slider effect whose matchName is 'ADBE Slider Control-0001' OR 'ADBE Slider Control' is pre-selected on the controller layer, it is used directly without renaming or confirmation.
The script searches for an existing controller by the name pattern '<layerName> || Time Control' before prompting to create a new one.
The endUndoGroup() call is placed after win.show() returns (modal dialog), so it fires after all user interactions including the OK click.

scripts.name: Centralizer

consumes.minimum: 1 active comp + 1 selected layer (ShapeLayer with ≥1 path, TextLayer, or AVLayer)
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem and its first selected layer. For ShapeLayer: traverses Contents to extract all ADBE Vector Group and ADBE Vector Shape-Group properties into a tree for UI display; user picks a path via the tree view, then reads that path's vertex points at the current time to compute bounding-box coordinates. For TextLayer: calls sourceRectAtTime to determine bounding rect. For AVLayer: reads layer.width, layer.height, layer.scale, and layer.position to derive bounds. Also reads layer.anchorPoint when adding anchor-center guides.

controls.role: when checked, adds two guides at the computed path centroid (centerX, centerY)

harvest evaluated slider values (post-expression) and adjust for layer scale
add composition-level guides based on checked options: center guides at anchor point, bounding-box guides at min/max X/Y, and bbox-center guides at computed centroid

CTRL/Effects/Distribute Along the Path/First Layer Index
CTRL/Effects/Distribute Along the Path/Last Layer Index

expressions.target: Item A/Transform/Position

sim.note: Sweeps Start Distribution Offset from 0 to 10 (pixels along path); output is the 2D position on the path for 'Item A' (index 2, first of 3 distributed layers). As startOffset increases, the distributed position slides forward along the path. The effective path length shrinks by the same amount.

sim.note: Sweeps Rotation Offset from -180 to +180 degrees; output is the rotation of 'Item A' in degrees, which is the path tangent angle at its position plus 90 degrees plus the offset. Curve is linear (y = pathTangentAngle + 90 + x) for a fixed path position.

notes: Script is HEADLESS — it runs inline with no ScriptUI window; all controls are pseudo-effect sliders on the generated CTRL null layer, manipulated from the AE Effects panel.
The same expression string is applied to both Position and Rotation of every distributed layer; the expression branches on thisProperty.name to return pos or rotation respectively.

Item A/Transform/Rotation

notes: HEADLESS script — no ScriptUI window; screenshot and uiTreePath are null.

Expression curves computed analytically: path API (pointOnPath, normalOnPath) is not evaluable in the harness expr-eval sandbox since the expression branches on thisProperty.name inside an if-block (last statement is not a bare expression), and relies on AE mask path runtime methods.

Position curve (curve 0): sweeps Start Distribution Offset 0-10 px using a reference 500 px horizontal line. Item A is the first distributed layer (myIndex=0), so its t-parameter equals startOffset exactly; x-coordinate of position = startOffset value. Curve is linear: y = x.
Rotation curve (curve 1): sweeps Rotation Offset -180 to +180 deg; on a horizontal reference path (tangent angle = 0 deg), rotation = 90 + offsetRotation, giving a linear y = 90 + x curve.
randomRotation is zeroed in both simulations to produce a deterministic curve (in live AE the Math.random() call causes per-frame flicker).

The harness ScriptUI runtime models treeview as a flat list with no .parent, so the Create handler always takes the 'Please select a shape from the tree view' alert branch and the null creation / expression writing code is never reached under the harness — only observable in a live AE session.

The position expression on vertex/tangent nulls uses 'value;' as a no-op fallback when Control/Follow is 0 (Control mode) or when the vertexIndex is out of range, preserving whatever position the null is manually set to.

Tangent null layers (inTangent, outTangent) are parented to the vertex null in createControls() but their position expressions are commented-out in the current source; they rely entirely on parent-child inheritance for positioning.
The path expression enumerates ALL layers in the comp on every frame (two nested loops), which can be slow in comps with many layers.
The shapeName embedded in the path expression is the shape group name (selectedItem.parent.text), not the path item name; naming collision is possible if multiple shape groups share the same prefix.
All coordinate transforms account for shapeGroup Transform Position minus Anchor Point offset to correctly map between shape-group local space, layer space, and comp space.
The Open/Closed Path toggle is implemented as an ADBE Checkbox Control effect on the vertex null; its value is passed directly as the fourth argument of createPath().

createPath() is an AE expression built-in that returns a new Shape value; it is not available in standard JavaScript — the expressions field captures the source as written for the AE ExtendScript / expression engine.

computes a uniform-random value for each dimension using startRange + Math.random() * (endRange − startRange)
applies the random value to every selected keyframe on every selected property via property.setValueAtKey
wraps all keyframe mutations in a single undo group labelled 'RandoMatic'

scriptSim.scenario: success – randomise 2D Position keyframes in range
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Rando Comp' (1920×1080, 24fps, 5s); one Shape layer with a 2D TwoD Position property ('Shape Layer 1/Transform/Position') carrying 3 keyframes at t=0, 1.5, 3 s — all three selected. Dialog range inputs: Start Range X=−50, End Range X=50, Start Range Y=−30, End Range Y=30. OK clicked.

notes: The number of range-input rows is dynamic: 1 row for scalar properties, 2 rows for 2D (e.g. Position), 3 rows for 3D (e.g. Position on a 3D layer). Controls Y and Z only appear when maxDimensionality >= 2 / 3.
Randomisation uses a uniform distribution (Math.random()). Actual keyframe values are non-deterministic; the harness can only assert operation kind and target, not exact values.
The script preserves keyframe timing and interpolation — only the value at each keyframe index is changed via setValueAtKey.
Input validation alerts inline and keeps the dialog open; it does not close on a bad-input OK click.

scripts.name: Triminator

consumes.minimum: 1 active composition + 1 selected shape layer
consumes.description: Reads the active CompItem and its selectedLayers array. For each shape layer (matchName 'ADBE Vector Layer'), reads layer.selectedProperties to collect NAMED_GROUP properties; if none, targets the layer's 'ADBE Root Vectors Group'. Also reads ScriptUI.environment.keyboardState.shiftKey at startup to determine whether keyframes should be written.

functions: detect active composition and guard against missing comp or empty selection
skip non-shape layers (matchName !== 'ADBE Vector Layer') silently
collect selected NAMED_GROUP properties from each shape layer to determine target groups
add a Trim Paths property ('ADBE Vector Filter - Trim') to each target group's contents
when Shift key is not held, write keyframes on Trim Start and Trim End animating from 0% to 100% over the first second of the layer
wrap all mutations in a single undo group named 'Add Trim Paths'

scriptSim.scenario: success – add Trim Paths with keyframes to root vectors group
scriptSim.inputs: Active comp 'Trim Comp' (1920×1080, 10 s, 24 fps); one Shape Layer selected ('Shape Layer 1', inPoint=0, outPoint=10); no shape groups selected; Shift key not held (addKeys=true)

operations.target: Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim Start
Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim Start
Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim End
Shape Layer 1/Contents/ADBE Vector Filter - Trim/ADBE Vector Trim End

notes: HEADLESS: no ScriptUI dialog is shown; the script runs immediately when invoked.
Shift-key detection uses ScriptUI.environment.keyboardState.shiftKey evaluated at startup; holding Shift suppresses all keyframe generation.
Only layers whose matchName equals 'ADBE Vector Layer' (shape layers) are processed; all other layer types are silently skipped.

When no shape groups are selected, Trim Paths is applied to the layer's root vectors group ('ADBE Root Vectors Group'); when groups are selected only those groups' contents ('ADBE Vectors Group') are targeted.
Keyframes animate from 0% to 100% at times [layer.inPoint, layer.inPoint + 1] — a fixed 1-second ramp starting at the layer's in-point.
Both Trim Start and Trim End receive identical keyframe sets (0→100), creating a simultaneous reveal; downstream timing adjustment is expected.
The script is compatible with batch-selected layers and groups across a single pass.
